title: Find and Qualify the Right Customer
description: Master the lead qualification framework and understand the 4 gates of customer verification

sections.title: Find and Qualify the Right Customer

content.subtitle: The foundation of controlled sales growth

sections.title: Why Qualification Matters
sections.content: Qualification is not about saying 'no'—it's about saying 'yes' to the right opportunities at the right time. In the NxN framework, we qualify leads through 4 critical gates that ensure we're investing time and resources on customers who truly fit our business model.

stages.label: Company Identity
stages.description: Verify the organization is real, has legitimate commercial operations, and matches our business model. Check: official registration, financial stability, industry alignment.

stages.label: Stakeholder Fit
stages.description: Identify decision-makers and influencers. Confirm we have the right people at the table who can authorize contracts.

stages.label: Need & Fit
stages.description: Understand their business problem and confirm our solution solves it. This is where qualification gets personal—it's about their pain points, not our features.

stages.label: Viability
stages.description: Confirm the deal size, timeline, and budget make business sense. Does this opportunity fit our commercial model? Is the ROI positive?

content.description: Apply the 4 gates to real-world lead scenarios. For each lead, identify which gate(s) they fail and what action you'd take.

scenarios.title: The Ghost Company
scenarios.description: You get a lead from John at 'TechFlow Industries.' Company registration appears to be in a tax haven, financials are opaque, and their website is a basic template.
scenarios.question: Which gate does this fail? What's your next action?
scenarios.correctAnswer: Gate 1 (Company Identity). Request official corporate docs or decline further engagement.

scenarios.title: The Gatekeeper
scenarios.description: Sarah from a Fortune 500 company is excited about our solution. But she's a junior analyst with no budget authority, and she can't introduce you to the CFO or procurement lead.
scenarios.question: What's the issue here? How do you move forward?
scenarios.correctAnswer: Gate 2 (Stakeholder Fit). You need decision-makers. Help Sarah identify the right stakeholders or deprioritize this lead.

scenarios.title: The Feature Shopper
scenarios.description: Miguel loves your platform's advanced reporting. But when you ask about his current process and pain points, he becomes vague. He hasn't actually defined what problem he's solving.
scenarios.question: Which gate is at risk? What should you do?
scenarios.correctAnswer: Gate 3 (Need & Fit). Push back with curiosity. Don't proceed until Miguel articulates a clear, specific business problem.

mistakes.title: Qualifying Too Early
mistakes.description: Saying 'no' before you've done real discovery. You reject a lead because they don't fit your initial assumption, when deeper conversation reveals a perfect fit.
mistakes.whyHarmful: You miss good deals. You also damage relationships—rejecting a lead without genuine understanding feels like dismissal.

mistakes.title: Falling in Love with the Opportunity
mistakes.description: Ignoring red flags because the deal size looks good. You move forward even though Gate 1 or Gate 4 are failing.
mistakes.whyHarmful: You invest time and resources in deals that won't close. You damage your win rate and team morale.

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: The 4 gates of qualification create discipline and prevent wasted sales effort
Qualification is CRM + Sales + Leadership working together
A clear 'no' is better than false hope

keyTakeaways: The 4 gates of qualification create discipline and prevent wasted sales effort
Qualification is CRM + Sales + Leadership working together
A clear 'no' is better than false hope
